note: Authoritative endpoint spec = the MCP tools/list JSON Schema. Generated, do not hand-edit. Regenerate: cargo run --bin dump_mcp_schema.

tools.description: HTTP GET request — fetch a URL and return status + body + headers

timeout_ms.description: Per-call timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

tools.name: http_get

tools.description: HTTP POST request — send JSON or text body and return status + body + headers

body.description:
Request body. Strings sent as-is; JSON values serialized to JSON
with `application/json` Content-Type unless overridden in headers.

timeout_ms.description: Per-call timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000.

url.description: Full HTTPS URL to POST to.

tools.name: http_post

body.description: Markdown body after the frontmatter fence.

frontmatter.description: Frontmatter as a JSON object (gets serialised to YAML on disk).

tools.name: irisy_soul_set

tools.description: Report kernel health: uptime, registered LLM adapters, MCP server count

tools.description: Write a persistent key into per-mcp local storage

messages.description: Chat messages, OpenAI-shape. Roles: user | assistant | system.

tools.description: Describe the knowledge base as a queryable RecordSource: fields (path/title/tags/created/modified) and supported operators. Call before notes.query.
tools.name: notes_describe

tools.description: Query the knowledge base by tag/title/date with a structured filter/sort/group request (not a query string). Returns matching notes. Call notes.describe first.

Conjunction.description:
How a request's filters combine — table-independent, a genuine enum (never a
caller string). `And` (default) keeps the original semantics; `Or` passes a
row that satisfies any filter (an empty filter set always passes, both ways).

Operator.description:
Table-INDEPENDENT operator set — a genuine compile-time enum (ADR-002 §14.1).
Only the `field` of a `Filter` is a validated string; the operator never is.

inputSchema.description:
notes.query — a structured read over the knowledge base as a RecordSource
(ADR-002 §14: the SAME query contract as smart-table). Fields are
path/title/tags/created/modified — call `notes.describe` for the set.

group_by.description: Group keys applied in order; empty = no grouping.

subdir.description: Optional vault subdir to scope the scan (omit for the whole vault).

inputSchema.title: NotesQueryArgs

tools.name: notes_query

tools.description: Describe the LLM provider catalogue as a queryable RecordSource (fields: id/label/kind/models/ready/capabilities). Call before providers.query.
tools.name: providers_describe

tools.description: Query configured LLM providers by id/kind/ready/capabilities with a structured filter/sort/group request. Call providers.describe first.

Conjunction.description:
How a request's filters combine — table-independent, a genuine enum (never a
caller string). `And` (default) keeps the original semantics; `Or` passes a
row that satisfies any filter (an empty filter set always passes, both ways).

Operator.description:
Table-INDEPENDENT operator set — a genuine compile-time enum (ADR-002 §14.1).
Only the `field` of a `Filter` is a validated string; the operator never is.

inputSchema.description:
registry.query / providers.query — a structured read over a runtime
RecordSource (ADR-002 §14: same query contract, no vault path).

group_by.description: Group keys applied in order; empty = no grouping.

inputSchema.title: RuntimeQueryArgs

tools.name: providers_query

tools.description: Describe the installed-MCP registry as a queryable RecordSource (fields: id/name/version/description/tools). Call before registry.query.
tools.name: registry_describe

tools.description: Query installed MCP servers by id/name/tool-count with a structured filter/sort/group request. Call registry.describe first.

Conjunction.description:
How a request's filters combine — table-independent, a genuine enum (never a
caller string). `And` (default) keeps the original semantics; `Or` passes a
row that satisfies any filter (an empty filter set always passes, both ways).

Operator.description:
Table-INDEPENDENT operator set — a genuine compile-time enum (ADR-002 §14.1).
Only the `field` of a `Filter` is a validated string; the operator never is.

inputSchema.description:
registry.query / providers.query — a structured read over a runtime
RecordSource (ADR-002 §14: same query contract, no vault path).

group_by.description: Group keys applied in order; empty = no grouping.

inputSchema.title: RuntimeQueryArgs

tools.name: registry_query

tools.description: Add a grid or kanban view to a smart table (persisted in frontmatter, not the table body). kanban requires group_by (a field key).

ViewKind.description: A smart-table view kind (ADR-003 §6.2) — a fixed enum (table-independent).

inputSchema.description:
smart_table.add_view — persist a view (grid/kanban) into frontmatter `views`
(ADR-003 §6.2: view state is NOT data; it lives in frontmatter, never the
table body).

group_by.description: Field key to group/columnize by (required for kanban).

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

inputSchema.title: SmartTableAddViewArgs

tools.name: smart_table_add_view

tools.description: Append a row to a smart table (values keyed by field key).

inputSchema.description: smart_table.append_row — produce/write a new row (ADR-002 §14 produce verb).

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

values.description: Cell values keyed by schema field key; missing keys become empty.

inputSchema.title: SmartTableAppendRowArgs

tools.name: smart_table_append_row

tools.description: Describe a smart table: its fields, types, and supported query operators. Call this before smart_table.query.
tools.name: smart_table_describe

tools.description: Query a smart table with a structured filter/sort/group request (not a query string). Call smart_table.describe first to learn valid fields.

Conjunction.description:
How a request's filters combine — table-independent, a genuine enum (never a
caller string). `And` (default) keeps the original semantics; `Or` passes a
row that satisfies any filter (an empty filter set always passes, both ways).

Operator.description:
Table-INDEPENDENT operator set — a genuine compile-time enum (ADR-002 §14.1).
Only the `field` of a `Filter` is a validated string; the operator never is.

inputSchema.description:
smart_table.query — a structured read over a smart-table RecordSource
(ADR-002 §14 / ADR-003 §6.5). Fill the parameter object; do NOT write a
query string. Call `smart_table.describe` first to learn the valid fields.

filters.description: Field filters, combined per `conjunction`. Each `field` must exist.

group_by.description:
Group keys applied in order (first is the primary level); equal-valued
rows are made contiguous. Empty = no grouping.

limit.description: Cap the number of returned rows (match_count is reported pre-limit).

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

inputSchema.title: SmartTableQueryArgs

tools.name: smart_table_query

tools.description: Run an AI field shortcut down a column: per row, classify/extract/summarize/translate/generate using {field} tokens, then write results into target_field. Cost-gated at 100 rows (pass confirm_over_gate=true to exceed). Skips already-filled cells unless force=true.

AiOp.description: The AI operation kind — a fixed, table-independent enum (ADR-002 §14.1).

inputSchema.description:
smart_table.run_ai_column — the AI field shortcut (ADR-003 §6.5.4): run an
LLM per row down a target column. Cost-gated at 100 rows.

confirm_over_gate.description: Confirm a run over the 100-row cost gate.

force.description: Re-run rows whose target cell is already filled (default false = resume).

op.description: The AI operation: classify / extract / summarize / translate / generate.

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

prompt.description: Prompt template; use `{field}` tokens to reference other columns.

target_field.description: Schema field key whose cells the AI fills.

inputSchema.title: SmartTableRunAiColumnArgs

tools.name: smart_table_run_ai_column

tools.description: Cancel an in-flight AI-column job by id (already-written cells are kept).

inputSchema.description: Reference an in-flight AI-column job by id (status / cancel).
inputSchema.title: JobIdArgs

tools.name: smart_table_run_ai_column_cancel

tools.description: Start an async AI field-shortcut job over a column (classify/extract/summarize/translate/generate, {field} tokens). Cost-gated at 100 rows. Returns a job_id; poll smart_table.run_ai_column_status.

AiOp.description: The AI operation kind — a fixed, table-independent enum (ADR-002 §14.1).

inputSchema.description:
smart_table.run_ai_column — the AI field shortcut (ADR-003 §6.5.4): run an
LLM per row down a target column. Cost-gated at 100 rows.

confirm_over_gate.description: Confirm a run over the 100-row cost gate.

force.description: Re-run rows whose target cell is already filled (default false = resume).

op.description: The AI operation: classify / extract / summarize / translate / generate.

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

prompt.description: Prompt template; use `{field}` tokens to reference other columns.

target_field.description: Schema field key whose cells the AI fills.

inputSchema.title: SmartTableRunAiColumnArgs

tools.name: smart_table_run_ai_column_start

tools.description: Get the status of an AI-column job: phase, rows_done/total, rows_written, errors.

inputSchema.description: Reference an in-flight AI-column job by id (status / cancel).
inputSchema.title: JobIdArgs

tools.name: smart_table_run_ai_column_status

tools.description: Set one cell of a smart table by row index + field key, then write it back.

inputSchema.description: smart_table.update_cell — produce/write one cell (ADR-002 §14 produce verb).

path.description: Vault-relative path to the smart-table `.md` file.

value.description: New cell value (stored as plain text).

inputSchema.title: SmartTableUpdateCellArgs

tools.name: smart_table_update_cell

tools.description: Read the frontmatter aliases list for a vault note
tools.name: vault_aliases

tools.description: Backlinks for a vault note (paths + snippets)
tools.name: vault_backlinks

tools.description: List vault outgoing links that point at no existing note (broken links)
tools.name: vault_broken_links

tools.description: Create a vault subdirectory (mkdir -p semantics)
tools.name: vault_create_folder

tools.description: Delete a vault note (the file is removed; no soft-delete)
tools.name: vault_delete

tools.description: Embed a single vault note into the local embeddings index
tools.name: vault_embed_note

tools.description: Snapshot of the vault embedding index (available / total / embedded / stale)
tools.name: vault_embedding_status

tools.description: Return the entire vault link graph (nodes + edges)
tools.name: vault_graph_data

tools.description: List markdown files under a vault subdirectory (or vault root)

subdir.description: Optional subdirectory relative to vault root. Absent = list root.

tools.name: vault_list

tools.description: Find unlinked mentions of text across the vault (excludes [[wikilinked]] hits)
tools.name: vault_mentions

tools.description: Move a vault note to a new path (alias of vault.rename)
tools.name: vault_move

tools.description: List notes tagged with a specific tag
tools.name: vault_notes_by_tag

tools.description: List vault notes that no other note links to
tools.name: vault_orphans

tools.description: Read a markdown file from the user's vault
tools.name: vault_read

tools.description: Rebuild the FTS5 vault search index from disk (returns indexed file count)
tools.name: vault_rebuild_index

tools.description: Re-embed all vault notes (bulk; respects content_hash unless force=true)
tools.name: vault_reembed_all

tools.description: Rename a vault note to a new path (no inbound-link rewrite)
tools.name: vault_rename

tools.description: Return the absolute vault root path on disk
tools.name: vault_root_path

tools.description: Full-text search the vault (FTS5 when available, substring fallback)

limit.description: Max results. Defaults to 20 if absent.

query.description: FTS5 query string. Falls back to substring scan when FTS unavailable.

tools.name: vault_search

tools.description: Semantic-similarity vault search (cosine over local embeddings)
tools.name: vault_semantic_search

tools.description: Toggle the starred flag on a vault note's frontmatter
tools.name: vault_set_starred

tools.description: Count un-integrated items in the sourcing inbox
tools.name: vault_sourcing_pending

tools.description: Run the kernel sourcing routine for the given YYYY-MM-DD date and write the review-queue file
tools.name: vault_sourcing_run

tools.description: Suggest related notes for a given path (embeddings-based autolink)
tools.name: vault_suggest_links

tools.description: List every tag in the vault with usage count (descending)
tools.name: vault_tags

tools.description: Describe the vault full-text source: source_kind=text; query content with a Contains filter whose value is the search needle. Call before vault_text_query.
tools.name: vault_text_describe

tools.description: Full-text query the vault as a §14 source: pass a Contains filter (field 'content', value = search text); returns matching note paths. Call vault_text_describe first.

Operator.description:
Table-INDEPENDENT operator set — a genuine compile-time enum (ADR-002 §14.1).
Only the `field` of a `Filter` is a validated string; the operator never is.

inputSchema.description:
vault_text.query args — the §14 Text profile of the vault (ADR-002 §14
TextSource). Only a `Contains` filter (the full-text needle) + `limit` are
meaningful; `describe` advertises exactly that.
inputSchema.title: VaultTextQueryArgs

tools.name: vault_text_query

tools.description: Drain recent vault filesystem events since a millis cursor (lazy-starts watcher)

prefix.description: Vault-relative prefix filter, e.g. `sourcing/`. Empty = all.

since_ms.description:
Unix epoch milliseconds cursor. Caller persists the last
`ts_ms` it saw and passes it back on the next poll.

tools.name: vault_watch

tools.description: Write a markdown file to the user's vault (creates parents)

body.description: Markdown body (without frontmatter; pass frontmatter via `frontmatter`).

frontmatter.description: Optional YAML frontmatter as a JSON object. Empty / absent = no frontmatter.

path.description: Vault-relative path. Parent dirs are created.

tools.name: vault_write

tools.description: Write a binary image asset to the vault (optionally with sidecar .md frontmatter)

path.description:
Vault-relative path for the binary asset (e.g.
`assets/images/2026-06-03/screenshot.png`).

sidecar_markdown.description:
Optional companion sidecar markdown (e.g. prompt / source URL).
When present, written alongside as `<path>.md` with frontmatter
(matching the kernel's `write_binary` + sidecar contract).

tools.name: vault_write_image
